meta.name: 100-Mile Trail Ultra
meta.subtitle: The ultimate — 100 miles across 36 weeks
meta.description: A 36-week plan for the full 100 miles. The most demanding training plan in the NextSplit library. Built around progressive back-to-back long runs, night running practice, crew coordination, and full-race nutrition rehearsal. Assumes completion of at least a 50-mile ultra first.
meta.coach_notes: One hundred miles will take most runners 24–30 hours. You will run through the night. You will reach moments when stopping seems entirely rational. The training can prepare your legs — it cannot fully prepare your mind. Train with your crew. Practise your drop bags. Know your aid station strategy better than your 5k race pace.

weeks.title: Week 1
weeks.note: Opening block. Build time on feet — pace is irrelevant.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

sessions.det: Walk every uphill · Eat at every aid station · Move through the night · Keep forward motion at all costs · The finish is the only goal

nut.d: Large meal 3 hours before. Porridge, banana, nut butter, toast. 800ml water. This is just the beginning of 24+ hours of fuelling.
60–80g carbohydrates per hour. Mix gels and real food from the start. Your ability to absorb nutrition will degrade after 12–15 hours — front-load your fuelling.
At crew points: change socks if wet, assess blisters, eat real food (broth, soup, potatoes, pizza), take 5–10 minutes to reset mentally. Do not sit too long.
Warm food is psychologically vital at night. Broth, ramen, soup at every night station. Caffeine gel or tablet every 3–4 hours through the night.
If solid food is impossible, switch to cola, gels, and broth. Continue electrolytes. The goal is calories in any form.
Eat slowly and consistently for 48 hours. 40g protein at first opportunity. Sleep as soon as medically safe. Continue drinking electrolytes for 24 hours. You have completed something extraordinary.
